title: Samuel Schapiro
text:
E-mail
  
  
    
     LinkedIn
  
  
    
     GitHub
  
  
      Google Scholar
  
  
    
     Bookshelf
  

  
  


I am an interdisciplinary scholar studying the science of intelligence and the creativity of AI systems. I’m interested in uncovering the fundamental, scientific principles of creativity governing minds and machines, and in developing AI methods and systems that exhibit creative behavior.
Previously, I was a visiting graduate researcher at the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing, Berkeley, and did my undergrad at the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign, where I studied theoretical computer science.





May 2026

New preprint: Assessing the Creativity of Large Language Models: Testing, Limits, and New Frontiers introduces the DRAT, the first creativity test that significantly predicts LLM scientific ideation.



May 2026

Launched the Creative AI Index, a community benchmark for AI creativity.



April 2026

Received the Lambda AI Research Grant.



September 2025

Best Short Paper Award at ICCC 2025 for Transformational Creativity in Science: A Graphical Theory.



August 2024

Started as a visiting graduate researcher at the Simons Institute, UC Berkeley.



May 2024

Pivoted to cognitive science — writeup.

title: Transformational Creativity in Science: A Graphical Theory
text:
Abstract
Creative processes are typically divided into three types: combinatorial, exploratory, and transformational. Here, we provide a graphical theory of transformational scientific creativity, synthesizing Boden’s insight that transformational creativity arises from changes in the “enabling constraints” of a conceptual space and Kuhn’s structure of scientific revolutions as resulting from paradigm shifts. We prove that modifications made to axioms of our graphical model have the most transformative potential and then illustrate how several historical instances of transformational creativity can be captured by our framework.

title: Transformational Creativity in Science: A Graphical Theory

title: Spark: A System for Scientifically Creative Idea Generation
text:
Abstract
Recently, large language models (LLMs) have shown promising abilities to generate novel research ideas in science, a direction which coincides with many foundational principles in computational creativity (CC). In light of these developments, we present an idea generation system named Spark that couples retrieval-augmented idea generation using LLMs with a reviewer model named Judge trained on 600K scientific reviews from OpenReview. Our work is both a system demonstration and intended to inspire other CC researchers to explore grounding the generation and evaluation of scientific ideas within foundational CC principles. To this end, we release the annotated dataset used to train Judge, inviting other researchers to explore the use of LLMs for idea generation and creative evaluations.

title: Spark: A System for Scientifically Creative Idea Generation

title: Deployment notes
text:
This repo hosts two sites at the same GitHub Pages output:







URL
Source




schapiro.ai/
Quarto-rendered from *.qmd → docs/


schapiro.ai/creative-ai-index/
Static Next.js export, committed to docs/creative-ai-index/



Both live under docs/ because GitHub Pages is configured to publish from main → /docs. The Creative AI Index export is committed directly to docs/creative-ai-index/ — it is not regenerated by quarto render.


Quarto’s website project type cleans output paths it doesn’t recognise. Since _quarto.yml’s project.resources does not list creative-ai-index/**, every quarto render deletes the entire docs/creative-ai-index/ subtree from the working tree.
The deletions are unstaged. The committed copy is still in HEAD, so the live site is fine — until you stage those deletions.
Why we don’t add creative-ai-index/** to resources: — it would force Quarto to copy ~500 files from the source-root creative-ai-index/ into docs/ on every render, taking 60+ seconds. The committed-directly approach is faster and keeps the Quarto build snappy.



You changed a .qmd, e.g. you added a quote to index.qmd.
quarto render

# DO NOT run `git add -A` here — it would stage the deletion of every
# file under docs/creative-ai-index/.

git add _quarto.yml \
        docs/*.html docs/search.json docs/sitemap.xml docs/styles.css \
        index.qmd styles.css \
        # ... any other rendered Quarto files you touched

git commit -m "Render: <what you changed>"
git push origin main
Use explicit paths. Never git add -A or git add . after a bare quarto render — the staged deletions would erase the Creative AI Index from the next commit, taking the subsite offline until the next deploy.
The working tree after this still has docs/creative-ai-index/ wiped on disk; that’s fine, it will be restored next time you run the deploy script for the Index.



You changed something in the creative-ai-index repo (a new metric, a UI tweak, etc.).
# In the creative-ai-index repo, NOT this one:
cd ~/Desktop/creative-ai-index
npm run deploy:schapiro
deploy:schapiro does three things in order: 1. next build with the static-export config → produces out/. 2. rm -rf then cp -R out/. ~/Desktop/samjschapiro2/creative-ai-index/ (source-root staging area). 3. cd ~/Desktop/samjschapiro2 && quarto render (regenerates Quarto pages).
Step 3 wipes docs/creative-ai-index/. Then you need to copy the fresh export from the source-root into docs/ manually:
cd ~/Desktop/samjschapiro2
rm -rf docs/creative-ai-index
cp -R creative-ai-index docs/creative-ai-index

git add docs/creative-ai-index docs/*.html docs/search.json docs/sitemap.xml docs/styles.css
git commit -m "Update creative-ai-index static export (<what changed>)"
git push origin main

TODO: fold the cp -R creative-ai-index docs/creative-ai-index step into deploy:schapiro itself, after the quarto render, so this becomes a single command from the creative-ai-index repo.




Don’t commit. Unstage the deletions, then continue with Workflow A:
git reset HEAD docs/creative-ai-index
git status   # confirm docs/creative-ai-index changes are no longer staged
If you’ve already committed the deletions but not pushed:
git reset --soft HEAD~1
git reset HEAD docs/creative-ai-index
# Re-stage what you actually wanted, then re-commit.
If you’ve already pushed, the live subsite is briefly offline — re-run Workflow B to restore it.




We tried project.resources: [creative-ai-index/**] in _quarto.yml to make Quarto copy the export into docs/ automatically. It works but slows every render to 60+ seconds because Quarto copies ~500 files on every invocation. Not worth it.
Committing the export directly under docs/creative-ai-index/ is the fastest path. The only cost is the discipline above.
